given_steps.step_text: Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000

when_steps.step_text: the clearing participant calculates the aggregated market-risk-component margin
the system applies rounding up to the nearest 10,000

then_steps.step_text: the aggregated margin before rounding is calculated as 46,929,904
the rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin is 46,930,000

given_steps.step_text: Aggregated margin before rounding is exactly 46,930,000
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000

when_steps.step_text: the system applies rounding up to the nearest 10,000

then_steps.step_text: the rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin remains 46,930,000
no upward adjustment is applied since value is already at the rounding boundary

given_steps.step_text: A clearing participant portfolio with margin calculation request

when_steps.step_text: the system validates the presence of all margin components
the system validates the rounding parameter from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

then_steps.step_text: Portfolio margin is present and valid
Flat rate margin is present and valid

Structured product add-on is present and valid
Corporate action position margin is present and valid
Holiday add-on is present and valid
Rounding parameter is retrieved from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

when_steps.step_text: the system calculates net margin after credit
the system applies the Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)] formula

then_steps.step_text: net margin after credit is calculated as 41,930,000

given_steps.step_text: Net margin before credit is exactly 5,000,000

when_steps.step_text: the system calculates net margin after credit
the system applies the Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)] formula

then_steps.step_text: the Maximum function ensures the result is not negative

given_steps.step_text: A clearing participant with margin calculation request

when_steps.step_text: the system validates the margin credit value
the system validates the Maximum function logic for net margin calculation

then_steps.step_text: margin credit is a valid positive numeric value
the Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)] formula is correctly implemented
